traditions.name: Charter Geometry
traditions.magicTexture: cold geometric light, exact notation, repeatable civic craft

traditions.name: Reed Memory
traditions.magicTexture: water holding names, woven reeds, patient shelter
traditions.costTexture: wet footprints, surrendered memories, debts to a household

traditions.name: Crystal Song
traditions.magicTexture: stored radiance released through exact music
traditions.costTexture: cracked heirlooms, lost notes, brilliance held too long

traditions.name: Bone Song
traditions.magicTexture: whalebone resonance, carved deeds, ale-warm chorus
traditions.costTexture: a true story, a broken carving, a debt to the dead

traditions.name: Blood Oath
traditions.magicTexture: living heat, first blood, witnessed promises
traditions.costTexture: scars, public shame, an oath that must be kept

traditions.name: Woven Thread
traditions.magicTexture: meaning caught in hems, knots, scarves, and civic rumor
traditions.costTexture: unraveled secrets, stained cloth, favors owed in public

traditions.name: Artisan Letters
traditions.magicTexture: written grace, canal reflections, meticulous finished work
traditions.costTexture: spoiled manuscripts, broken etiquette, beauty surrendered

traditions.name: Horse-Singing
traditions.magicTexture: low songs understood by horses and carried through grass
traditions.costTexture: a prized mount's trust, a long road, a secret overheard

traditions.name: Ancestor Culture
traditions.magicTexture: living cultures carrying the strengths of generations
traditions.costTexture: a personal strength passed on, clan obligation, ritual appetite

traditions.name: Gontark Curse-Craft
traditions.magicTexture: sharp names, horn-scratched prohibitions, patient malice
traditions.costTexture: reciprocal misfortune, a god's notice, a reputation for murder

traditions.name: Sound and Ink
traditions.magicTexture: rhythm made visible in ink and carried between camps
traditions.costTexture: lost verses, altered tattoos, thirst, a road abandoned

traditions.name: Tidecraft
traditions.magicTexture: salt bargains, code-switched names, knots tested by weather
traditions.costTexture: cargo cast overboard, a season away, favors in three ports

regions.name: Marble Containment Yard

affordances.text: Marble walls create hard lines of sight and official blind spots.

regions.voiceSummary: Imperial Vigovia under marble law: censors measure and label every wonder, and boots move in unison. Keep outcomes cold, precise, and civic.
ambientLines: You hear the rhythmic stamp of boots in unison.
A horn sounds three precise notes, then falls silent.
Iron scrapes against iron in perfect time.

bases.name: survey marker
bases.description: Its measured grooves divide even empty air into accountable portions.

bases.name: evidence table
bases.description: Wire squares indicate where confiscated wonders are supposed to stop being dangerous.

bases.name: permit box
bases.description: Narrow slots accept precisely the forms that no prisoner here possesses.

bases.name: inspection lamp
bases.description: Its white charter-light leaves no flattering shadows.

materials.description: The stone is clean enough to make dirt feel accused.
Every fastening bears a maintenance date and an approving initial.
Cold geometry waits inside the glass for an authorized hand.

conditions.description: A black file number has been renewed often enough to emboss the surface.
A red seal states that possession has been regularized.
Some former stain survives only as a different shine.
One attached docket plainly belongs to a different object and nobody has corrected it.

hooks.title: Notice affixed to {name}
hooks.text: NOTICE IS GIVEN that {name} remains subject to inspection under the current containment schedule.

regions.name: Vigovian Capital

affordances.text: Capital defenses turn patrols, warrants, and court procedure into layers that can be spent down or worked around.
Emperor Odran is a living actor inside the same world rules as everyone else.

regions.voiceSummary: The Vigovian capital: ceremonial marble, reasonable and afraid, where even the throne obeys the law it wrote. Keep outcomes formal, watched, and precise.
ambientLines: Somewhere a censor's bell marks the hour, exactly.
A petition cart rattles over marble worn smooth by orderly disappointment.
A horn sounds three precise notes, then falls silent.

bases.name: petition desk
bases.description: Its writing slope has been polished by generations of reasonable desperation.

bases.name: queue rail
bases.description: It arranges waiting bodies into the shape preferred by the state.

bases.name: tariff scale
bases.description: Twin pans weigh goods against obligations that are not quite coin.

bases.name: archive trolley
bases.description: Drawers of ribboned files wait to be returned to rooms that have moved.

bases.name: civic fountain
bases.description: The water rises in licensed parabolas and never splashes the posted code.

materials.description: The marble carries a faint gray grain like handwriting under paint.
Stamped serials make every replaceable part individually accountable.
Thin official skins have been pasted over older repairs.
Perfect lines of sleeping charter-light cross beneath the surface.

conditions.description: A current docket explains its purpose in language that avoids every active verb.
A nearly invisible fracture has acquired three inspection signatures.
Red wax declares an unknown former owner no longer relevant.
Fingers have left a dark crescent where citizens steady themselves before asking.
The shine is so complete that touching it feels like entering a record.

hooks.title: Filed text on {name}
hooks.text: By order of {ruler}, {name} is assigned to {region} for orderly public use until superseded.

regions.name: Hollowmere Margin

affordances.text: Reed beds give fugitives soft cover and water-flavored spell hooks.
Hollowmere folk may hear anti-imperial stories, but shelter is a household risk rather than a slogan.

regions.voiceSummary: Frontier country under imperial eyes: reeds, market roads, old shrines, and buried strata of older magic. Keep outcomes earthy and vivid, wonder with mud on its boots.
ambientLines: Something unseen brushes the rushes, curious.
A reed door knocks twice in still air; someone inside waits before answering.
Far off, water finds a new way down.

bases.name: memory loom
bases.description: Wet warp-threads hold household names until somebody sings them loose.

bases.name: eel trap-basket
bases.description: The funnel mouth is clever enough to look innocent from inside.

bases.name: ferry oath-post
bases.description: Notches record crossings, favors, and the names of those the ferryman refused.

bases.name: household water-shrine
bases.description: A shallow bowl offers the house's cleanest memory to whatever comes thirsty.

bases.name: reed drying-rack
bases.description: Bundles hang in an order that distinguishes roofing, medicine, and hiding places.

materials.description: Fresh-cut reeds give off a green pepper smell when bent.
Cloudy river-glass flashes silver wherever a hand last touched it.
Rust blooms in maps that local children use for directions.
Pale withies flex instead of breaking and remember the shape afterward.

conditions.description: Last flood's fine gray fur remains in every sheltered seam.
Small knotted favors ask for dry sleep, safe kin, and soldiers looking elsewhere.
A lead occupation tag hangs awkwardly from the folk workmanship.
Water in one hollow keeps tapping the first four notes of a work song.
A careful repair uses conspicuously Vintan thread and nobody admits why.

hooks.title: Names kept by {name}
hooks.text: The marks on {name} remember a dry crossing east and a household that still owes shelter.

regions.name: Stalnazan Singing Heights

affordances.text: Charged crystals can preserve one working for another hand or another hour.
Court music, chosen succession, and the celebrated first regicide make legitimacy a local instrument.

regions.voiceSummary: Stalnaz speaks through resonance, winter light, and patient refinement. Beauty is public power here, and every crystal may be holding somebody else's unfinished note.
ambientLines: The crystal road gives back one footstep in harmony.
A window keeps yesterday's violet light after the sun has moved on.
Two distant musicians tune to the same note without seeing one another.

bases.name: resonance bench
bases.description: Its graduated hollows let a tuner compare a crystal's answer against the mountain.

bases.name: light cistern
bases.description: Faceted walls collect spare daylight for evening rehearsals.

bases.name: heiress plinth
bases.description: An empty name-panel waits with impeccable patience for the queen's eventual choice.

bases.name: snow harp
bases.description: Mountain wind plays it badly but with enormous confidence.

materials.description: Deep blue facets store a cool afterimage of every nearby gesture.
Fine mountain stone rings softly instead of cracking under cold.
Hair-thin wire carries resonance between parts that never touch.

conditions.description: Yesterday's amber light has not quite finished leaving it.
A court tuner signed the underside with one precise musical phrase.
Frost gathers only along the notes the object prefers.
A fine fracture is being observed rather than repaired, as though it may resolve into art.

hooks.title: Tuning inscription on {name}
hooks.text: The inscription records a charge in C-sharp, reserved for the next public need of {region}.

regions.name: Brall Whaleholds

affordances.text: Worked whalebone holds vibration, carved stories, and old martial pride.
A witnessed deed can grow quickly when each teller improves somebody else's part.

regions.voiceSummary: Brall is whalebone, salt, ale, and stories told louder for the benefit of the person beside you. Keep it warm, physical, and generous even when pride has teeth.
ambientLines: A carved roof-rib hums when the harbor wind changes.
Someone in the next hold improves a story you have not heard yet.
Ale foam runs along a scrimshaw groove and completes the pictured wave.

bases.name: harpoon winch
bases.description: The drum is broad enough to haul a whale or an especially stubborn story.

bases.name: tale-bench
bases.description: Each seat-back depicts a different witness improving the same impossible event.

bases.name: scrimshaw tally-post
bases.description: Tiny carved scenes account for debts through heroic exaggeration.

bases.name: ale-cooling rack
bases.description: Sea wind passes through wet slats and leaves the casks sweating cold.

materials.description: Old bone answers knocks with a note too deep for its size.
Black pitch keeps salt out and old handprints in.
The metal is green at the edges and bright where nervous hands rub it.

conditions.description: A sweet brown tide-line marks several evenings nobody recalls consistently.
A local hero appears twice their proper height and deservedly so.
An imperial tag certifies that no prohibited human bone was used.
A trapped harbor note swells whenever clouds gather offshore.

hooks.title: Carving on {name}
hooks.text: The carving claims three strangers once lifted {name} together, though each figure is plainly somebody local.

regions.name: Ryolan Racefields

affordances.text: Promises made before a crowd carry more leverage and more danger than private assurances.
Team loyalties cross households and districts, offering instant fellowship or an avoidable fight.

regions.voiceSummary: Ryolan is blunt ceremony over hot blood: first cuts, chosen teams, public oaths, and arguments that become duels before anyone calls them quarrels.
ambientLines: A chariot crowd roars beyond the ridge, then groans as one body.
Fresh palm-blood darkens the dust beside a carefully witnessed oath.
Two strangers compare team colors before deciding whether to be civil.

bases.name: oath-stone
bases.description: A shallow palm hollow faces the crowd rather than the speaker.

bases.name: chariot rail
bases.description: Team colors overlap where supporters repaint it after disputed victories.

bases.name: duelist's weapon-rack
bases.description: Paired slots keep every blade beside the weapon chosen against it.

bases.name: first-blood basin
bases.description: Clean water waits to receive the cut that makes a challenge public.

materials.description: Iron-rich stone darkens almost black when wet.
Scraped bronze carries paint from half the realm's teams.
The hide is stamped with names of witnesses rather than makers.

conditions.description: Fresh color declares a loyalty older paint refuses to surrender.
One dark thumbprint has been left deliberately unwashed.
A knotted cord shows that somebody's promise remains unsettled.
The damage maps exactly where spectators leaned during a famous finish.

hooks.title: Witnessed oath on {name}
hooks.text: Before the people of {region}, the marked hand swore to return what was taken before the next race.

regions.name: Vintan Weaveways

affordances.text: Scarves, awnings, ledgers, and swaddles may carry dormant protections or listening knots.
Vint's separatists argue in public, turning rumor and policy into usable social terrain.

regions.voiceSummary: Vint is quick, sly, and civic: gossip caught in awning-knots, policy weighed on brass scales, and every ordinary object implicated in somebody's argument.
ambientLines: A red-thread debt ledger gains a new knot while its owner looks away.
Three vendors repeat the same rumor with three incompatible improvements.
An anti-eavesdropping awning shivers whenever anyone says the treasurer's name.

bases.name: gossip awning
bases.description: Charm-knots along the hem tighten whenever a familiar scandal passes underneath.

bases.name: debt-ledger stand
bases.description: Threads cross the open pages between names that insist they are unrelated.

bases.name: tariff post
bases.description: Imperial tags and republican corrections compete for the last bare inch.

bases.name: woven-charm rack
bases.description: Scarves promise warmth, privacy, fertility, revenge, and very limited refunds.

bases.name: rumor-scale
bases.description: One pan holds coin; the other holds sealed slips whose weight changes by listener.

materials.description: Civic red thread has been knotted into arguments, repairs, and quiet accusations.
The brass is bright where generations checked the measure for cheating.
Different weaves make nearby speech feel private or dangerously public.

conditions.description: A legal party sigil has been stitched where occupation clerks must pretend not to mind it.
Each new knot claims to correct the rumor represented by the last.
Layered fees tell the recent history of three governments and one unchanged policy.
Collected rain makes the woven charms mutter indiscreetly.

hooks.title: Threaded note on {name}
hooks.text: Someone stitched that the current minister promised reform in {region}; a second hand added that the minister never specified which direction.

regions.name: Canals of Threen

affordances.text: Threenians and imperial emissaries maintain independence as a shared courtesy with many exploitable seams.
Workshops turn local craft into finished goods, commissions, services, and trade routes.

regions.voiceSummary: Threen is literary and mannered, all canal light and finished craft, performing a beautiful independence that every courteous person knows not to examine too loudly.
ambientLines: A bookseller ferries one sealed poem across a canal by itself.
An imperial emissary bows first; the Threenian official makes a study of accepting.
Workshop hammers keep time with a recital in the room above.

bases.name: poem ferry-box
bases.description: A tiny cable-ferry carries sealed pages between facing windows.

bases.name: finisher's bench
bases.description: Shallow drawers hold the last beautiful parts that turn work into export.

bases.name: courtesy screen
bases.description: Painted reeds let two officials disagree without visibly sharing a room.

bases.name: canal oath-bollard
bases.description: Boat ropes share the post with ribbons from promises made before departure.

materials.description: Glaze catches canal light and returns it improved.
Patient layers hide every join except the one the maker meant you to admire.
Lines of miniature text ornament surfaces too useful for a whole book.

conditions.description: A small imperial seal has been placed lower than protocol strictly requires.
Rippling light makes the workmanship appear to revise itself.
A popular line has been copied here with one politically meaningful word changed.
Fine cord and oilcloth wait for a buyer who has not yet arrived.

hooks.title: Canal verse on {name}
hooks.text: The verse praises independent {realm} in a meter traditionally reserved for polite surrender.

regions.name: Monteary Grasslands

affordances.text: A trusted singer may speak with a horse, but never safely within an imperial witness's hearing.
The realm's stallions and breeding records are protected more fiercely than most treasuries.

regions.voiceSummary: Monteary is grass, wind, guarded bloodlines, and plain people who notice how a stranger treats a horse before caring what title they claim.
ambientLines: A gelding turns one ear toward a song too low for you to hear.
The grass lies down in a broad road where no cart has passed.
A breeder closes the stallion ledger when imperial brass flashes on the road.

bases.name: horse-singing post
bases.description: Teeth marks ring the height where quiet songs are usually offered.

bases.name: bloodline gate
bases.description: A hidden latch separates public pasture from jealously guarded ancestry.

bases.name: journey saddle-rack
bases.description: Each peg bears the distance of the road its saddle is meant to survive.

bases.name: wind-water trough
bases.description: Grass seeds circle the surface without crossing its center.

materials.description: Soft hide has been rubbed dark by muzzles and reins.
Pale local wood smells of crushed hay when warmed.
A private maker's mark resembles no official registry.

conditions.description: Long black hairs remain braided around one corner.
A permit plate has been turned carefully toward the wall.
Dust from several soils names a journey more honestly than the owner does.
The lower edge shines from animals choosing the same comfortable turn.

hooks.title: Breeder's mark on {name}
hooks.text: The private mark records a mare sold north and a stallion that officially never left {region}.

regions.name: Ontrian Clanlands

affordances.text: Each clan keeps a distinct living yoghurt culture that carries inherited strengths and obligations.
Chartered shamans preserve public rites while private households remember older freedoms.

regions.voiceSummary: Ontria is communal and ancestral: clay crocks kept alive longer than dynasties, high herbs, clan hospitality, and strengths understood as loans from the dead.
ambientLines: A clay crock is greeted by name before any living guest.
Children argue over which ancestor made the rain smell of thyme.
A shaman checks an imperial seal, then performs the older gesture underneath it.

bases.name: clan culture-crock
bases.description: The living culture inside is addressed as an elder and fed before guests.

bases.name: strength-sharing spoon
bases.description: Many tooth marks testify to generations of serious ceremonial tasting.

bases.name: high-herb rack
bases.description: Mountain herbs hang in clan-specific orders no outsider reads correctly.

bases.name: ancestor stool
bases.description: Nobody living sits here, though it is repaired more carefully than the others.

materials.description: Smoke-black clay retains the warmth of meals long finished.
The grain releases a clean herbal smell under a thumbnail.
Clan colors have faded differently on every wrapped layer.

conditions.description: A dead relative's everyday name appears where a maker's mark would be.
An official seal permits the public rite and says nothing about the private one.
A faint pulse of fermentation makes nearby dust tremble.
The break has been mended with clay saved from the previous vessel.

hooks.title: Clan names on {name}
hooks.text: The names record who gave strength to the clan and one person whose contribution was refused.

regions.name: Gontark Crags

affordances.text: A curse is dangerous social force as well as magic; offering one for pay marks the speaker like a hired killer.
Narrow crag paths reward climbing, forced movement, and bodies built for bad footing.

regions.voiceSummary: Gontark is spare and sharp: horned people on pale crags, their own gods close at hand, and curses treated with the sober fear due any murder weapon.
ambientLines: Hooves click along a path that appears too narrow for a shadow.
A horn-scratched warning names no victim, which is worse.
Someone leaves milk at a cliff shrine and does not look over the edge.

bases.name: curse-warning post
bases.description: Scratched prohibitions explain everything except who was warned.

bases.name: cliff milk-shrine
bases.description: A shallow horn cup faces the drop and is never found empty by morning.

bases.name: thorn-path gate
bases.description: Interlocked thorn canes admit horns more readily than shoulders.

bases.name: horn-worker's anvil
bases.description: Its curved face fits tools made for materials other smiths avoid naming.

materials.description: Thin stone flakes keep sharp writing longer than paint.
Polished horn reflects faces with the eyes slightly wrong.
Every cut branch has grown one last defensive hook.

conditions.description: Three notches invoke a local god and a fourth appears to argue.
A deliberate empty space is more threatening than a written name.
Repeated offerings have left a clean mineral skin.
An official seal has been removed with patient, technically non-magical violence.

hooks.title: Warning cut into {name}
hooks.text: The warning forbids payment for curses and advises debtors to fear gifts offered without a price.

regions.name: Parn Sunroads

affordances.text: Tattoo lines can carry rhythm, memory, identity, or magic without revealing which until used.
People, services, stock, and rumors travel in bands rather than waiting in fixed settlements.

regions.voiceSummary: The Parn sunroads are rhythmic and road-worn: ochre distances, music kept against thirst, and tattooed stories moving wherever the next camp can be made.
ambientLines: A caravan song arrives before the dust that carries it.
Blue tattoo ink darkens when the singer finds the road's old key.
Tent bells answer one another across a distance the map calls empty.

bases.name: caravan song-post
bases.description: Small tuned holes let the desert wind announce which road meets here.

bases.name: tattoo-ink screen
bases.description: Dyed hide panels keep sun, grit, and uninvited meanings out of fresh ink.

bases.name: road tent-bell
bases.description: Its note identifies a caravan band before faces become visible.

bases.name: water-share tally
bases.description: Sliding beads divide a skin of water among travelers, animals, and obligations.

materials.description: Hot brass gives off a clear note without being struck.
Layered blue lines look almost black until rhythm passes nearby.
Road-soft hide carries repairs from more camps than the maker knew.

conditions.description: One repeated phrase makes the whole object answer in harmony.
The dune has begun claiming it without yet interrupting its use.
Overlapping band signs show hospitality, warning, and an old marriage route.
An official blessing sits beside older ink and neither admits dependence.

hooks.title: Road verse on {name}
hooks.text: The verse names a well two nights west, then changes key where the safe road turns south.

regions.name: Rentacosta Harbor

affordances.text: Almost everyone needs Rentacosta's ships, making the harbor useful neutral ground and violence unusually costly.
Cold bargains with merfolk bring rare goods, difficult etiquette, and routes no land map can show.

regions.voiceSummary: Rentacosta is easygoing and salt-worn without being soft: multilingual docks, long absences, dangerous voyages, and a practical welcome for anyone who can keep a bargain.
ambientLines: A dockside argument changes languages twice and ends in shared laughter.
Merfolk silver lies under wet canvas while three brokers pretend not to watch it.
A child chalks return dates beside ships already late by a season.

bases.name: tide cargo-crane
bases.description: Counterweights are marked for cargo from three seas and one depth nobody names aloud.

bases.name: harbor language-board
bases.description: Arrival notices share space in six scripts and several expressive drawings.

bases.name: merfolk bargain-scale
bases.description: One pan is always wet and the other refuses iron coin.

bases.name: voyage rope-rack
bases.description: Knots sort lines by storm, cargo, depth, and whether the ship is expected home.

bases.name: return-date post
bases.description: Chalk dates accumulate beside names without anyone erasing the impossible ones.

materials.description: Gray wood has swallowed enough brine to outlast its nails.
Cold silver beads seawater into tiny lenses.
Tarred fibers carry the smells of distant ports in separate layers.

conditions.description: Instructions begin in one language, joke in another, and become serious in a third.
The highest salt line comes from a storm locals still use as a date.
Fresh chalk waits beside an old return date nobody calls overdue.
Several hostile flags have each added the same protected harbor sign.

hooks.title: Harbor notices on {name}
hooks.text: Notices list a northbound ferry, a merfolk exchange at low tide, and a ship three months late from Brall.

regions.name: Wild Border

affordances.text: Wild terrain makes transformations feel close at hand while attention and cost remain hard to predict.

regions.voiceSummary: Deep wild country: dreamlike, gently impossible, jewel-bright. Light lingers, glass grows, and distances disagree. Describe outcomes with strange, vivid beauty.
ambientLines: A voice hums a lullaby with no breath behind it.
Your shadow arrives half a step late.
Somewhere near, a festival is being remembered by the stones.

bases.name: broken-law stone
bases.description: An obsolete rule is carved on one face and contradicted by the flowers growing through it.

bases.name: rain altar
bases.description: Several kinds of weather have left offerings here for one another.

bases.name: color snare
bases.description: Loose loops catch hues shed by passing birds, spells, and arguments.

bases.name: remembered festival-post
bases.description: Faded ribbons dance to music the air has not played today.

bases.name: boneflower bed
bases.description: White stems click together and turn their blossoms toward spoken names.

materials.description: Porous white material drinks falling water and releases it upward later.
Jewel glass keeps colors belonging to celebrations that never happened here.
The grain shifts slowly enough to deny movement while never matching yesterday.

conditions.description: Its shadow completes each motion after the object has stopped.
Small flowers open from every hollow whenever someone lies nearby.
Rain wets one side while warm dust collects on the other.
Scratches gather into almost-legible names when nobody looks directly.

hooks.title: Words growing on {name}
hooks.text: The living letters say the nearest honest road begins wherever the reader stops pretending to be lost.
